controls.details: Verify the internal audit charter is approved by the board, grants unrestricted access, and establishes organisational independence.
controls.acceptance: Signed charter on file, dated within 2 years, no management reporting line for the CAE.

controls.details: Confirm a formal risk-based plan is produced annually, approved by the audit committee, and tracked for completion.
controls.acceptance: Plan approved by audit committee, coverage of top-10 risks, quarterly status reports on file.

controls.details: Each audit engagement must have a documented scope, objectives, criteria, and resource plan before fieldwork begins.
controls.acceptance: Planning memo completed and reviewed prior to fieldwork; scope sign-off by auditee management.

controls.details: All audit findings must be tracked from issuance through management response to verified closure.
controls.acceptance: Tracker current within 30 days; Critical/High findings with due dates; no overdue items without documented extension.

controls.details: Internal audit must maintain a QAIP including ongoing monitoring and periodic internal/external assessments.
controls.acceptance: External assessment within 5 years; internal assessment annual; results communicated to senior management and board.

controls.details: Final audit reports must be issued in a timely manner with management responses and agreed action plans.
controls.acceptance: Reports issued within 30 days of fieldwork completion; management responses within 15 business days.

controls.details: Evaluate whether a formal, documented process exists for requesting, approving, and provisioning user access to systems.
controls.acceptance: Written procedure in place; access requests approved by data owner; provisioning logs retained for 12 months.

controls.details: All user accounts in in-scope systems must be reviewed at least semi-annually for continued business need.
controls.acceptance: Review completed within the last 6 months; revocations actioned within 5 business days; documented sign-off by managers.

controls.details: Privileged (admin) accounts must be inventoried, approved, and their usage logged and reviewed.
controls.acceptance: Inventory current; each privileged account tied to a named individual; activity reviewed monthly.

controls.details: Conflicting roles (e.g. create payment + approve payment) must be identified and either separated or mitigated with compensating controls.
controls.acceptance: SOD matrix documented; conflicts <5% of user population; compensating controls evidenced for all approved conflicts.

controls.details: Accounts of terminated employees must be disabled/removed within 24 hours of HR notification.
controls.acceptance: Sample of 20 leavers; all accounts disabled same day or next business day; no active accounts for leavers.

controls.details: MFA must be enforced for all remote access, privileged accounts, and cloud management consoles.
controls.acceptance: MFA enabled for 100% of remote-access users and admins; configuration screenshots on file.

controls.details: A formally approved information security policy must exist, be communicated to all staff, and reviewed annually.
controls.acceptance: Policy approved by board/senior management; review within 12 months; distribution records on file.

controls.details: Organisation's controls must be mapped to applicable frameworks (ISO 27001, SOC 2, PCI-DSS, NIST CSF).
controls.acceptance: Mapping document current; gaps identified and tracked; owner assigned per control.

controls.details: All employees must complete security awareness training at least annually; records retained.
controls.acceptance: ≥95% completion rate; training updated within 12 months; phishing simulation results documented.

controls.details: Regular vulnerability scans must be conducted and findings remediated within SLA (Critical: 7 days, High: 30 days).
controls.acceptance: Scans run at least monthly; no overdue Critical/High findings; remediation tracked to closure.

controls.details: All changes to production systems must follow a documented change management process with approval and rollback plans.
controls.acceptance: Change log complete; emergency changes <10% of total; unauthorised changes: zero tolerance.

controls.details: A documented and tested incident response plan must be in place covering detection, containment, eradication, and recovery.
controls.acceptance: Plan reviewed within 12 months; tabletop exercise completed annually; contact lists current.

controls.details: All critical systems (servers, firewalls, endpoints, applications) must forward logs to a centralised SIEM or log platform.
controls.acceptance: Log sources inventoried; >95% of critical assets forwarding logs; latency <5 minutes.

controls.details: Logs must be retained for the period required by policy and applicable regulations (minimum 12 months online, 7 years archive).
controls.acceptance: Retention configuration matches policy; sample log retrieval test successful for 12-month-old events.

controls.details: SIEM alerts must be triaged within defined SLAs; all Critical alerts investigated and closed within 4 hours.
controls.acceptance: Alert queue reviewed; mean-time-to-triage <4 hours for Critical; no unacknowledged alerts >24 hours.

controls.details: Log storage must be protected from modification; write-once storage or cryptographic hashing required.
controls.acceptance: Log integrity mechanism in place; sample hash verification test passed; admin access to log store reviewed.

controls.details: All privileged (admin) activities must be logged, including commands executed, files accessed, and configuration changes.
controls.acceptance: Privileged session logging enabled on all admin interfaces; logs reviewed monthly.

controls.details: Anomaly detection rules must be reviewed and tuned quarterly to reduce false positives and maintain detection fidelity.
controls.acceptance: Rules reviewed within 90 days; false positive rate <20%; tuning log on file.

controls.details: A complete, classified inventory of all third-party vendors with access to systems or data must be maintained.
controls.acceptance: Inventory current within 90 days; vendors classified by risk tier; data classification noted.

controls.details: Risk-tiered due diligence assessments (questionnaire, SOC 2 review, on-site) must be completed before onboarding.
controls.acceptance: Assessment on file for all Tier-1/2 vendors; completed within 12 months of last review.

controls.details: A Data Processing Agreement (DPA) must be in place with all vendors processing personal or confidential data.
controls.acceptance: DPA register complete; 100% coverage for data-handling vendors; DPAs reviewed by legal.

controls.details: All vendor contracts must include minimum security clauses: breach notification, right-to-audit, subprocessor controls.
controls.acceptance: Security clause checklist completed for all contracts; gaps remediated at next renewal.

controls.details: Ongoing monitoring of Tier-1 vendors must include quarterly reviews of SLA performance and security posture.
controls.acceptance: Monitoring log current; escalation process documented; annual re-assessment for Tier-1 vendors.

controls.details: Critical software and hardware supply chains must be mapped to identify concentration risks and single points of failure.
controls.acceptance: Map covers all Tier-1 vendors; subprocessors identified; concentration risks assessed.

controls.details: Firewall rulesets must be reviewed annually; rules must have documented business justification and owner.
controls.acceptance: Review completed within 12 months; no 'any-any' rules; rule count justified; unused rules removed.

controls.details: Critical zones (PCI, production, DMZ, management) must be separated and validated against documented architecture.
controls.acceptance: Diagram current; segmentation verified by configuration review; inter-zone traffic policy documented.

controls.details: Wireless networks must use WPA3 (or WPA2 minimum), separate guest networks, and rogue AP detection.
controls.acceptance: WPA2+ confirmed; guest network isolated; rogue AP detection active; last assessment within 12 months.

controls.details: External and internal penetration tests must be conducted annually; critical findings remediated within 30 days.
controls.acceptance: Test within 12 months by qualified third party; critical findings closed; retest evidence on file.

controls.details: Network devices must be configured against CIS or vendor hardening benchmarks; default credentials changed.
controls.acceptance: Hardening baseline documented; sample configuration review passed; no default credentials in use.

controls.details: Threat intelligence feeds must be integrated with network controls (firewall, IPS) and reviewed for actionable indicators.
controls.acceptance: At least one curated feed active; indicators actioned within 24 hours; review log on file.

controls.details: A Record of Processing Activities must be maintained covering all personal data flows, lawful bases, and retention periods.
controls.acceptance: RoPA current within 6 months; all processing activities documented; data flows mapped.

controls.details: Privacy notices must meet applicable legal requirements (GDPR, CCPA) and be presented at point of data collection.
controls.acceptance: Legal review completed within 12 months; all required elements present; notices visible at collection points.

controls.details: Consent records must be captured, stored, and be revocable; consent withdrawal must be processed within 30 days.
controls.acceptance: Consent records auditable; withdrawal mechanism functional; processing halted within 30 days of withdrawal.

controls.details: Processes for handling Subject Access Requests, erasure requests, and portability requests must exist and meet legal timelines.
controls.acceptance: DSR log maintained; 100% of requests responded to within legal deadline; no overdue requests.

controls.details: PIAs must be completed for all new or significantly changed processing activities involving high-risk personal data.
controls.acceptance: PIA register current; PIAs completed before go-live; DPO sign-off on file.

controls.details: A documented data breach response procedure must exist; breaches must be notified to regulators within 72 hours where required.
controls.acceptance: Procedure approved; breach log maintained; notification timeline met for all incidents in review period.

controls.details: A complete hardware asset inventory must be maintained and reconciled against physical counts at least annually.
controls.acceptance: Inventory current within 90 days; physical count reconciliation <2% discrepancy; assets labelled.

controls.details: Access to data centre and server rooms must be restricted, logged, and reviewed; no tailgating controls in place.
controls.acceptance: Access list reviewed within 6 months; badge log retained 12 months; anti-tailgate measures active.

controls.details: Firmware versions for servers, network devices, and endpoints must be tracked against approved baselines and patched within SLA.
controls.acceptance: Baseline documented; no devices >2 patch cycles behind; critical firmware patches within 30 days.

controls.details: Storage media must be securely wiped or destroyed before disposal; certificates of destruction retained.
controls.acceptance: Disposal log complete; certificates on file for all destroyed media; NIST 800-88 or equivalent method used.

controls.details: Temperature, humidity, fire suppression, and UPS systems in data centres must be monitored and maintained.
controls.acceptance: Environmental monitoring active; alerts configured; last maintenance within 12 months; UPS tested annually.

controls.details: All corporate mobile devices and laptops must be enrolled in MDM/UEM with encryption, remote wipe, and compliance policy enforced.
controls.acceptance: 100% enrolment; encryption enforced; non-compliant devices blocked; lost device remote-wipe tested.

controls.details: A documented SDLC must include security requirements gathering, threat modelling, code review, and security testing gates.
controls.acceptance: SDLC policy current; security checkpoints documented; evidence of gates applied to last 3 releases.

controls.details: SAST tools must be integrated into CI/CD pipelines; builds with Critical/High findings must not promote to production.
controls.acceptance: SAST integrated; gate policy enforced; no Critical findings in production release; scan history retained.

controls.details: Software Composition Analysis must identify vulnerable third-party libraries; remediation SLAs enforced.
controls.acceptance: SCA tool active in pipeline; no Critical CVEs in production; SBOM generated per release.

controls.details: Web applications must be tested against OWASP Top-10 vulnerabilities at least annually; findings tracked to remediation.
controls.acceptance: Test within 12 months; no unmitigated Critical/High OWASP findings; retest evidence on file.

controls.details: APIs must be inventoried, authenticated (OAuth 2.0 / API keys), rate-limited, and tested for OWASP API Top-10 issues.
controls.acceptance: API inventory current; authentication enforced on all external APIs; rate limiting active; test within 12 months.

controls.details: Secrets (API keys, passwords, certificates) must never be stored in source code; a secrets manager must be used.
controls.acceptance: No secrets in code repositories (verified by scan); secrets manager deployed; rotation policy enforced.
